news.summary: Boston, on June 12, 2025 - Fapon Biopharma, a company in the clinical-stage biotech sector specializing in developing therapeutic antibodies and fusion proteins, has disclosed its involvement in the BIO International Convention 2025 (BIO 2025) in Boston. The convention is scheduled to occur from June 16 to June 19 at the Boston Convention center.

news.summary: Through Ericsson On-Demand, there is a substantial change in the deployment, management, and scalability of core network services. This service allows Communication Service Providers (CSPs) to quickly provision core services within minutes, enabling effortless scaling and pay-as-you-go model based on usage. By merging telecom-grade reliability with cutting-edge technology, Ericsson On-Demand is revolutionizing the way CSPs operate.
news.headline: Ericsson and Google Cloud partner for 5G core as-a-service with AI. Ericsson On-Demand lets CSPs scale network services quickly and pay per use.

news.summary: China has been recognized with two global water awards for their advancements in sustainable desalination technology. This achievement took place in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The awards mark a significant milestone in China's efforts to address water scarcity issues, with their innovations making a notable impact.
news.headline: China receives 2 global water awards in sustainable desalination, highlighting innovations in the field.

news.summary: Established in 2023, the ABS Digital Art Prize is recognized as the premier award for on-chain digital artists. The Artist of the Year 2025 title was jointly awarded to Anna Ridler and Sofia Crespo, while the Emerging Artist of the Year 2025 was revealed to be Cezar Mocan. This marks a significant milestone in the digital art world, with these talented individuals paving the way for future creators in this rapidly evolving field.
news.headline: Anna Ridler & Sofia Crespo win Arab Bank Switzerland's Digital Art Prize 2025. Founded in 2023, the prize celebrates on-chain digital artists.

news.summary: On June 12, 2025, Cerillion (AIM: CER), a prominent company offering composable BSS/OSS solutions in the telecoms sector, revealed its significant involvement in TM Forum's main event, DTW25 – Ignite, set to happen from June 17-19 in Copenhagen. Cerillion, a Silver sponsor for the event, will be participating to showcase its capabilities in the industry.
news.headline: Cerillion (AIM: CER) to participate in TM Forum's DTW25 - Ignite event in Copenhagen from 17-19 June.

news.summary: The ThermoLoop ™ technology developed by the company aims to substitute costly electrolyzers and has the potential to disrupt a market valued at $12 trillion by introducing abundant and cost-effective green hydrogen.
news.headline: NewHydrogen aims to disrupt $12 trillion market with ThermoLoop™ tech to produce cheapest green hydrogen.

news.summary: Experienced market expert Lawrence McDonald has issued a strong caution regarding a recent proposal from the GOP led by Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas). The proposal aims to cease federal interest payments to banks. McDonald, renowned as the founder of The Bear Traps Report, shared this warning on a platform, citing a post by Bloomberg journalist Steven Dennis. Dennis shared a fact sheet outlining Cruz's plan to eliminate the interest rate on the reserve balances (IORB) program initiated in 2008. Cruz argues that this policy costs taxpayers over $100 billion per year and has accrued a total cost of approximately $1.1 trillion, with almost half of it benefiting foreign financial institutions. For more details, the complete story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Market expert Lawrence McDonald warns of financial repression as Sen. Ted Cruz aims to end $1.1 trillion Fed interest payment scheme, costing taxpayers over $100 billion annually.

news.summary: In Dakar, Senegal, as of June 12, 2025, Guinean artist and activist Elie Kamano has unveiled an impactful music video for his latest single titled 'Ils veulent l'Afrique sans les Africains' ('They Want Africa Without Africans'). Through this release, Kamano seeks to convey a strong message blending art and political commentary, highlighting issues surrounding Africa.
news.headline: Elie Kamano releases music video 'Ils veulent l'Afrique sans les Africains', amplifying Pan-African reparations call.

news.summary: Former NBA star Shaquille O'Neal, also known as Shaq, has agreed to pay $1.8 million to settle claims that he endorsed unregistered securities linked to the collapsed cryptocurrency exchange FTX. The settlement, which was filed in Florida federal court, does not require O'Neal to admit any wrongdoing. This deal stands out as one of the initial instances where a well-known celebrity has resolved a legal dispute related to FTX's bankruptcy in November 2022. Plaintiffs alleged that O'Neal promoted FTX as a reliable investment platform during live events and on social media. The class action lawsuit pertains to investors who put money into FTX or possessed its FTT token from May 2019 onwards. For more details, you can find the complete article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: NBA Legend Shaq settles FTX class action for $1.8M without admitting fault in crypto promotion case.

The company aims to enhance its presence in the changing hybrid work landscape by delivering innovative products and services.

news.summary: Today, Moon Surgical, a top company in Physical AI for surgical procedures based in France and the United States, revealed its involvement at NVIDIA GTC Paris in conjunction with VivaTech 2025. Through partnering with NVIDIA, Moon Surgical is showcasing the Maestro System, solidifying its position as an industry leader.

news.summary: Michael Saylor recently revealed that the pandemic-induced lockdowns and continuous money printing led him to convert his company's substantial cash reserves into Bitcoin. This decision was primarily influenced by the unprecedented circumstances, with lockdowns affecting businesses worldwide and money printing causing concerns over inflation. Saylor emphasized that the move was essential to safeguard the company's assets amid the economic uncertainties brought about by these events.
news.headline: Michael Saylor attributes shift to Bitcoin to COVID-19 disruptions and money printing in lockdown, converting company's reserve.

news.summary: In a significant decision, the 78th Session of the United Nations General Assembly convened on June 7, 2024, and approved a resolution put forth by China. This resolution designated June 10 as the International Day for Dialogue Among Civilizations. The inaugural edition of this event was observed globally on June 10, 2025.
news.headline: UNGA adopted China's resolution on June 7, 2024, designating June 10 as International Day for Dialogue Among Civilizations.

news.summary: The integration of Greater Than's AI-generated risk intelligence into Smart Eye's latest version of the AIS system has enabled the prediction of crashes in advance, enhancing risk management for fleet and transport operators. This upgraded system is specifically tailored for aftermarket installation in commercial vehicles. The development was unveiled in Stockholm in June, offering advanced capabilities to enhance safety on the road.
news.headline: Greater Than & Smart Eye enhance partnership to offer predictive risk scoring in upgraded driver support system, aiding fleet operators.
